goal: Add a bounded SPG-005 permission and tool exposure projection contract so runtime cards can show sandbox, approval, network, visible/deferred/hidden tool classes, key tool names, and blocked permission attempts without becoming a second tool registry or claim-support surface.

forbiddenFiles: src/lib/delivery-truth/** except tests that prove orientation-only behavior if required

designConstraints: Tool exposure is orientation and audit-trail evidence only in this slice; it cannot support delivery, merge-ready, goal-ready, or judge/pm-ready claims.

The packet must be pointer-only and must not store secrets, raw tool call arguments, full MCP manifests, path lists, command fragments, user-provided argument text, or hidden tool names that the runtime did not expose.
Runtime-card projection must summarize counts/classes and key names without embedding bulky registries or duplicating the command capability catalog.
Blocked permission attempts must use a closed reason taxonomy, bounded operator-safe message text, and evidence refs only; free-form raw evidence strings are forbidden.
Key tool names must be capped and deterministically truncated so the runtime card cannot grow into a de facto command or tool registry.
If a standalone tool-exposure-snapshot/v1 schema is added, the schema, example, manifest entry, and runtimeStatus/blockedBy fields are required in the same slice.
Permission blocked, tool unavailable, tool not attempted, and claim failed must remain distinct states in fixtures and validation errors.
Existing prompt-context receipt permission posture remains valid; this slice adds runtime-card projection semantics rather than replacing prompt-context orientation receipts.
acceptanceCriteria: ToolExposureSnapshot/v1 validates sandbox mode, approval policy, network posture, visible/deferred/hidden tool class counts, key tool names, and blocked permission attempts.

Validation rejects claim-support evidence use, raw tool call payload fields, secret-like values, path lists, command fragments, user-provided argument text, negative counts, unknown statuses without failureClass, and blocked permission attempts without closed reason/evidenceRef.
Validation rejects uncapped key tool names, overlong key-name lists, and summaries whose truncation metadata does not match the original count.
Runtime-card codexRuntime projection admits a compact toolExposure summary and rejects summaries whose blocked/unavailable counts are inconsistent with blocker counts.
Runtime-card fixture or command evidence proves an agent can discover codexRuntime.toolExposure through the existing runtime-card JSON output path.
Delivery-truth or equivalent verifier negative fixture rejects any toolExposure-derived ref used as claim support with an orientation-only failure class.
Runtime-card fixtures distinguish tool unavailable, permission blocked, not attempted, and claim failed without collapsing them into one failure class.
No public command behavior, pr-closeout behavior, or delivery-truth claim support changes in this slice.
rollbackPlan: Remove the optional codexRuntime.toolExposure projection and validation paths.
Remove tool-exposure schema/example/manifest entries if added.
Preserve prior prompt-context, runtime-card, delivery-truth, and pr-closeout behavior.
stopConditions: Stop if implementation needs public command behavior or delivery-truth claim support before a separate reviewed intent expands scope.
Stop if the packet cannot distinguish unavailable tool, permission blocked, not attempted, and claim failed states.
Stop if validation requires storing raw tool call payloads, secrets, or full hidden tool inventories.
